items.title: S&P 500 closes lower, Nasdaq falls more than 1% as chip stocks suffer
items.content: S&P 500 lost 1.01% to 7,457.69, Nasdaq dropped 1.4% to 25,520.24, Dow fell 406.55 points (0.77%) to 52,146.42. Tech-led sell-off driven by deepening semiconductor rout.

items.title: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq post weekly losses as semiconductors get smoked
items.content: Dow fell ~0.7%, S&P 500 down 1%, Nasdaq down 1.4% on Friday after release of Moonshot AI's Kimi K3, the world's most powerful open AI model from China.

items.title: US stock market: S&P 500 at 7,494, down 0.52% on Jul 17
items.content: US500 fell to 7,494 points on July 17, losing 0.52%. Over the past month, index declined 0.08%, though remains 19% higher YoY. Losses led by Caterpillar, chip producers.

items.title: US Core Inflation falls to 2.6% in June, below expectations of 2.8%
items.content: Core CPI fell from 2.9% (May) to 2.6%, undershooting market estimates. CPI-U decreased 0.4% MoM in June, first decline since May 2020. Driven by easing shelter costs.

items.title: China's New AI Model Triggers Fresh Tech Selloff
items.content: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3 with 1M-token context window. Z.ai (formerly Zhipu) released GLM-5.2 open-weight model. 6 of top 10 AI models on developer platforms are now Chinese. Nasdaq 100 futures fell 2.3%.

items.title: China's AI Breakthrough Triggers Semiconductor Selloff
items.content: Selling in semiconductor stocks causing selloff in all tech stocks. Chinese firms plan to allocate 46% of AI accelerator budgets to domestic suppliers over next 12 months.

items.title: Hang Seng Climbs 1.33% to Over-One-Month High as Tech Rally Extends to Five Days
items.content: HSI hit over-one-month high with five-session winning streak, one of its strongest runs of 2026. Diverging from cautious tone elsewhere in Asia.

items.title: China's Economy Grows at Slowest Pace in Years
items.content: China Q2 GDP grew 4.3% YoY, missing both forecasts and annual target of 4.5-5%. H1 GDP at 4.7%. Broad slump outside export-oriented manufacturing.

items.title: China stocks close down on chip profit-taking
items.content: Chinese stocks ended slightly lower amid sell-off in semiconductor shares, as investors locked in recent gains. Shanghai composite closed 0.2% lower at 4,786.78.

items.title: US seeks free Hormuz access from Iran as talks focus on strait
items.content: Trump says US and Iran agree to continue ceasefire talks. US seeking guaranteed free navigation through Strait of Hormuz. Ongoing geopolitical uncertainty reducing risk appetite.

items.title: This Is a Forever War in the Making - US-Iran Strait of Hormuz
items.content: Analysis of US-Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz corridor. US views corridor as insurance against another Iranian closure.

items.title: Earnings Calendar for Week of July 20-24: Key reports ahead
items.content: Q2 earnings season in full swing. ASML YTD up 66.6% through July 14. Iran ceasefire likely came too late for many US companies to avoid Q2 impact.
